project.name: Lanes Ferry Landing
project.summary: Lanes Ferry Landing is a proposed revision to a very large mixed-use residential master development plan in Rocky Point. The county package describes about 976.58 acres around NC 210 and Moore Town Road, with proposed residential neighborhoods, commercial land, amenity centers, walking trails, parks, sidewalks, wetlands, floodplain areas, and access points from NC 210 and Moore Town Road. The 2025 package lists 2,420 single-family detached units, 125 attached or townhome units, 205 multifamily units, and about 10 acres for roughly 15,000 square feet of commercial use.
project.locationDescription: North and south of NC Highway 210 at Moore Town Road, about 1.8 miles east of I-40 Exit 408 in the Rocky Point District.
project.developer: HCT Pender, LLC; River Rock Farm, LLC; Springfield Plantation II, Inc.; C Heide Trask, Jr.
project.applicant: HCT Pender, LLC; River Rock Farm, LLC; Springfield Plantation II, Inc.; C Heide Trask, Jr.
project.owner: HCT Pender, LLC; River Rock Farm, LLC; Springfield Plantation II, Inc.; C Heide Trask, Jr.
project.latestUpdate: Pender County lists MDP 2025-86 Lanes Ferry as pending, last updated August 15, 2025.
project.nextStep: Verify current Planning Board or county action before publication, especially the final unit cap and any conditions attached to the 2025 master plan revision.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Overview
graphicsSuggestions.reason: The overall site plan contains the site information block, acreage, ownership, zoning, wetlands, floodplain labels, and the broad project layout.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Neighborhood Layout
graphicsSuggestions.reason: The detailed site plan pages show hundreds of lots, multifamily buildings, roads, and the internal layout.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Traffic & Access
graphicsSuggestions.reason: The plan labels NC Highway 210, Moore Town Road, East Essex Road, and private roads. User should choose the clearest access sheet in Graphics.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Environmental Features
graphicsSuggestions.reason: The plan labels wetlands, 100-year floodplain, 500-year floodplain, and floodway areas near the Northeast Cape Fear River.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Open Space & Recreation
graphicsSuggestions.reason: The narrative describes three resort-style amenity centers, walking trails, numerous neighborhood parks, public and private spaces, and sidewalks.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Mixed Use / Commercial Area
graphicsSuggestions.reason: The package describes about 10 acres for commercial use, and the site plan should be used to select the clearest commercial-area graphic.

graphicsSuggestions.label: Phasing
graphicsSuggestions.reason: The narrative identifies Phase 1 north of NC 210 and southern Phase 2 and Phase 3 tracts. User should choose a plan sheet if phase boundaries are visually clear.

communityConcerns.summary: The 2025 package lists a very large development program: 2,420 single-family detached units, 125 attached or townhome units, 205 multifamily units, and commercial land on about 976.58 acres.

The project surrounds NC 210 and Moore Town Road, with access points from NC 210 and Moore Town Road. Trip totals and required road improvements should be verified from traffic studies or county staff reports.
The site plan labels extensive wetlands, floodway, 100-year floodplain, and 500-year floodplain areas near the Northeast Cape Fear River.
The 2025 package proposes about 10 acres of commercial land for roughly 15,000 square feet of commercial use, along with multiple housing types and amenity sites.
The narrative says the project will include three resort-style amenity centers, walking trails, neighborhood parks, public and private spaces, tree-lined streets, and sidewalks.

sourceReferences.value: +/- 10 acres for +/- 15,000 sf of commercial use
PD, Planned Development and RA, Rural Agricultural
